{{Short description|none}}
 <!-- This short description is INTENTIONALLY "none" - please see WP:SDNONE before you consider changing it! -->
{{About|the men's team|the women's team|Liechtenstein women's national football team}}
{{Use dmy dates|date=November 2025}}
{{Infobox national football team
| Name              = Liechtenstein
| Badge             = Crown of Liechtenstein.svg
| Badge_size        = 200px
| Nickname          = ''Blau-Rot''<br>(The Blue-Reds)
| Association       = [[Liechtenstein Football Association|Liechtensteiner Fussballverband]] (LFV)
| Confederation     = [[UEFA]] (Europe)
| website           = [https://www.lfv.li/ lfv.li]
| Coach             = [[Konrad Fünfstück]]
| Captain           = [[Nicolas Hasler]]
| Most caps         = [[Peter Jehle]] (132)
| Top scorer        = [[Mario Frick (footballer)|Mario Frick]] (16)
| Home Stadium      = [[Rheinpark Stadion]]
| FIFA Trigramme    = LIE
| FIFA Rank         = {{FIFA World Rankings|LIE}}
| FIFA max          = 118
| FIFA max date     = January 2008, July 2011, September 2011
| FIFA min          = 206
| FIFA min date     = October 2025, April 2026 – June 2026
| Elo Rank          = {{World Football Elo Ratings|Liechtenstein}}
| Elo max           = 145
| Elo max date      = June 1984
| Elo min           = 183
| Elo min date      = June 2005
| pattern_la1       = _lie26h
| pattern_b1        = _lie26h
| pattern_ra1       = _lie26h
| pattern_sh1       = _lie26h
| pattern_so1       = 
| leftarm1          = 0000E0
| body1             = 0000E0
| rightarm1         = 0000E0
| shorts1           = FF0000
| socks1            = 0000E0
| pattern_la2       = _lie26a
| pattern_b2        = _lie26a
| pattern_ra2       = _lie26a
| pattern_sh2       = _lie26a
| pattern_so2       = 
| leftarm2          = FFFFFF
| body2             = FFFFFF
| rightarm2         = FFFFFF
| shorts2           = FFFFFF
| socks2            = FFFFFF
| pattern_la3       = 
| pattern_b3        = 
| pattern_ra3       = 
| pattern_sh3       =
| pattern_so3       =
| leftarm3          = 
| body3             = 
| rightarm3         = 
| shorts3           = 
| socks3            = 
| First game        = {{fb|LIE|1937}} 1–1 {{fb-rt|Malta}}<br>([[Daejeon]], [[South Korea]]; 14 June 1981)
| Largest win       = {{fb|LUX}} 0–4 {{fb-rt|LIE}}<br>([[Luxembourg City|Luxembourg]], [[Luxembourg]]; 13 October 2004)
| Largest loss      = {{fb|LIE}} 1–11 {{fb-rt|MKD|name=FYR Macedonia}}<br>([[Eschen]], [[Liechtenstein]]; 9 November 1996)
| World cup apps    = 
| Regional name     = [[UEFA European Championship|European Championship]]
| Regional cup apps = 
}}

The '''Liechtenstein national football team''' ({{langx|de|Liechtensteinische Fussballnationalmannschaft}}) is the national [[Association football|football]] team of the Principality of [[Liechtenstein]] and is controlled by the [[Liechtenstein Football Association]]. The organisation is known as the ''Liechtensteiner Fussballverband'' in [[German language|German]]. 

The team's first match was an unofficial match against [[Malta national football team|Malta]] in [[Seoul]], a 1–1 draw in 1981. Their first official match came two years later, a 0–1 defeat from [[Switzerland national football team|Switzerland]]. Liechtenstein's largest win, a 4–0 win over [[Luxembourg national football team|Luxembourg]] in a [[2006 FIFA World Cup]] qualifier on 13 October 2004, was both its first away win and its first win in any [[FIFA World Cup]] qualifier.

Conversely, Liechtenstein is the only country that has lost official matches against [[San Marino national football team|San Marino]] – one in a 2004 [[exhibition game|friendly]], and twice in the [[2024–25 UEFA Nations League|2024–25 UEFA Nations League]].<ref name="SMR">{{cite news|url=https://www.vaterland.li/liechtenstein/sport/nach-dem-var-entscheid-eingeknickt-art-575639|title=Nach dem VAR-Entscheid eingeknickt|language=German|publisher=[[Liechtensteiner Vaterland]]|date=5 September 2024}}</ref> Liechtenstein suffered its biggest ever loss in 1996, during [[1998 FIFA World Cup qualification (UEFA)|1998 FIFA World Cup qualifiers]], losing 1–11 to [[North Macedonia national football team|Macedonia]] (now North Macedonia), the result also being Macedonia's largest ever win to date. The team's head coach is currently [[Konrad Fünfstück]].<ref>{{Cite web |title=Konrad Fünfstück neuer Nationaltrainer |url=https://www.lfv.li/news?tx_news_pi1%5Baction%5D=detail&tx_news_pi1%5Bcontroller%5D=News&tx_news_pi1%5Bnews%5D=2043&cHash=2ad1237e8a986e5f653dcf8d1244e9c7 |language=de |access-date=23 May 2023 |archive-date=24 May 2023 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20230524045716/https://www.lfv.li/news?tx_news_pi1%5Baction%5D=detail&tx_news_pi1%5Bcontroller%5D=News&tx_news_pi1%5Bnews%5D=2043&cHash=2ad1237e8a986e5f653dcf8d1244e9c7 |url-status=live }}</ref>

==History==
Liechtenstein are only a relatively recent affiliate to [[FIFA]], and did not participate in any qualifying series until the [[UEFA Euro 1996]] qualifiers. There they managed to surprise the [[Republic of Ireland national football team|Republic of Ireland]] by holding them to a 0–0 draw on 3 June 1995. On 14 October 1998, they managed their first victory in a qualifying campaign after winning 2–1 against [[Azerbaijan national football team|Azerbaijan]] in a [[UEFA Euro 2000 qualifying]] match.

Since then, the presence of Liechtenstein clubs in the Swiss league system and of a handful of professional players (most notably [[Mario Frick (football player)|Mario Frick]]) has seen the side's competitiveness improve slightly. The [[UEFA Euro 2004 qualifying|Euro 2004 qualifiers]] saw Liechtenstein improve to the extent they restricted [[England national football team|England]] to 2–0 wins. Also at this time Liechtenstein lost 1–0 against [[San Marino national football team|San Marino]], considered to be the weakest national team. The [[2006 FIFA World Cup qualification (UEFA)|2006 FIFA World Cup qualifiers]] brought even better results as two wins over [[Luxembourg national football team|Luxembourg]] and draws against both [[Slovakia national football team|Slovakia]] and [[Portugal national football team|Portugal]] meant that Liechtenstein finished with 8 points.

In the [[UEFA Euro 2008 qualifying|UEFA Euro 2008 qualifiers]], Liechtenstein beat [[Latvia national football team|Latvia]] through a solitary goal from Mario Frick. The result caused the Latvian manager to resign after the match. They repeated their heroics against [[Iceland national football team|Iceland]] managing to beat them 3–0 on 17 October 2007 for their second qualifying group win. On 26 March 2008 Liechtenstein had an embarrassing 7–1 loss to fellow small nation in Europe, [[Malta national football team|Malta]]. This was recorded as Malta's largest win.<ref>{{Cite news|url=https://www.timesofmalta.com/articles/view/20080326/sport/malta-beat-liechtenstein-7-1.201675|title=Malta beat Liechtenstein 7-1|work=Times of Malta|access-date=2018-11-07|language=en-GB|archive-date=8 November 2018|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20181108065329/https://www.timesofmalta.com/articles/view/20080326/sport/malta-beat-liechtenstein-7-1.201675|url-status=live}}</ref>

The Liechtensteiner Fussballverbund voted [[Rainer Hasler]] to be their "Golden Player" &mdash; their best player over the last 50 years &mdash; to mark UEFA's golden jubilee.

In the [[2010 FIFA World Cup qualification|2010 FIFA World Cup qualifiers]], Liechtenstein secured a scoreless draw against [[Azerbaijan national football team|Azerbaijan]] and a 1–1 draw against [[Finland national football team|Finland]], finishing bottom of Group 4 on two points.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://eu-football.info/_match.php?id=1149|title=Liechtenstein and Finland football teams played to a 1:1 draw, 9 September 2009|website=eu-football.info|language=en|access-date=2018-11-07|archive-date=10 September 2018|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20180910061338/https://eu-football.info/_match.php?id=1149|url-status=live}}</ref>

In the [[UEFA Euro 2012 qualifying|UEFA Euro 2012 qualifiers]], Liechtenstein were narrowly beaten 2–1 by [[Scotland national football team|Scotland]] in [[Hampden Park]] thanks to a goal by [[Stephen McManus]] in the seventh minute of additional time.<ref name = "euro 2012">{{cite news|url=http://news.bbc.co.uk/sport1/hi/scotland/15078473.stm|title=Liechtenstein missing goal hero Philippe Erne|work=BBC Sport|publisher=BBC|date=27 September 2011|access-date=27 September 2011|archive-date=13 June 2024|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20240613111349/https://www.bbc.com/sport/scotland/15078473|url-status=live}}</ref> They produced a shock 2&ndash;0 win at home against [[Lithuania national football team|Lithuania]]; their goals were scored by [[Philippe Erne]] and [[Michele Polverino]].<ref name = "euro 2012"/> In the following qualifying game, they managed a scoreless draw away to Lithuania.<ref name = "euro 2012"/>

In the [[2014 FIFA World Cup qualification – UEFA Group G|qualifiers for the 2014 FIFA World Cup]], the ''Blue-Reds'' were humiliated at home in their opening game against [[Bosnia and Herzegovina national football team|Bosnia-Herzegovina]] (1–8).<ref>{{cite web |access-date=25 February 2015 |date=7 September 2012 |language=en |title=Records fall as Bosnia put eight past Liechtenstein |url=http://www.uefa.com/worldcup/season=2014/matches/round=2000294/match=2008709/postmatch/report/index.html |website=[[UEFA]] |archive-date=10 June 2017 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20170610092547/http://www.uefa.com/worldcup/season=2014/matches/round=2000294/match=2008709/postmatch/report/index.html |url-status=dead }}<!-- auto-translated from French by Module:CS1 translator --></ref> The team loses many of its qualifying matches<ref>{{cite web |access-date=25 February 2015 |language=de |title=Statistik A-Nationalmannschaft |url=http://www.lfv.li/no_cache/nationalmannschaften/a-nationalmannschaft/statistik.html |website=lfv.li |archive-date=9 October 2011 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20111009001020/http://www.lfv.li/no_cache/nationalmannschaften/a-nationalmannschaft/statistik.html |url-status=live }}<!-- auto-translated from French by Module:CS1 translator --></ref> and earns its only two points in draws against [[Latvia national football team|Latvia]]<ref>{{cite web |access-date=25 February 2015 |title=Le Liechtenstein concède le match nul face à la Lettonie (1-1) |url=http://www.eurosport.fr/football/world-cup-qualification-uefa/2014/le-liechtenstein-concede-le-match-nul-face-a-la-lettonie-1-1_sto3679118/story.shtml |website=eurosport.fr |archive-date=13 June 2024 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20240613111224/http://www.eurosport.fr/football/world-cup-qualification-uefa/2014/le-liechtenstein-concede-le-match-nul-face-a-la-lettonie-1-1_sto3679118/story.shtml |url-status=live }}<!-- auto-translated from French by Module:CS1 translator --></ref> and [[Slovakia national football team|Slovakia]].<ref>{{cite web |access-date=25 February 2015 |date=7 June 2013 |title=Le Liechtenstein tient la Slovaquie en échec |url=http://fr.uefa.com/worldcup/season=2014/matches/round=2000294/match=2008713/postmatch/report/ |website=[[UEFA]] |archive-date=13 June 2024 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20240613111407/https://fr.uefa.com/errors/ |url-status=dead }}<!-- auto-translated from French by Module:CS1 translator --></ref> The team finished bottom of its group.

For the [[UEFA Euro 2016 qualifying Group G|UEFA Euro 2016 qualifying campaign in France]], Liechtenstein managed to finish second-bottom of the group and take five points, managing a goalless draw at home against [[Montenegro national football team|Montenegro]],<ref>{{cite web |access-date=25 February 2015 |date=9 October 2014 |title=Foot/Euro-2016/Qualif. - Liechtenstein et Monténégro 0 à 0 |url=http://www.lematin.ch/sports/depeches/footeuro2016qualif--liechtenstein-montenegro-0-0/story/21990052 |archive-url=https://archive.today/20150225123402/http://www.lematin.ch/sports/depeches/footeuro2016qualif--liechtenstein-montenegro-0-0/story/21990052 |url-status=dead |archive-date=25 February 2015 |website=lematin.ch }}<!-- auto-translated from French by Module:CS1 translator --></ref> an away win against [[Moldova national football team|Moldova]], one goal to nil, thanks to [[Franz Burgmeier]]'s ninth goal<ref>{{cite web |access-date=25 February 2015 |date=15 November 2014 |title=Euro 2016 : Le Liechtenstein s'impose face à la Moldavie 1 but à 0 |url=https://fr.athlet.org/football/euro/2016/qualifications/groupe-g/2014-11-15-moldavie-liechtenstein.html |website=athlet.org |archive-date=13 June 2024 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20240613111231/https://fr.athlet.org/football/euro/2016/qualifications/groupe-g/2014-11-15-moldavie-liechtenstein.html |url-status=live }}<!-- auto-translated from French by Module:CS1 translator --></ref> and then a 1–1 home draw in the return against the same Moldovans, the group's red lanterns with three fewer units.

During the [[2018 FIFA World Cup qualification – UEFA Group G|2018 FIFA World Cup qualifiers]], The ''Blue-Reds'' lost all 10 of their matches and finished bottom of their group with no points scored and just one goal scored (away against [[Israel national football team|Israel]]) compared to 39 conceded.

In 2018, Liechtenstein entered the first [[UEFA Nations League]], in group 4 of league D.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.uefa.com/uefanationsleague/season=2019/standings/#/ld|title=UEFA Nations League - Standings|website=[[UEFA]]|language=en|access-date=2018-11-07|archive-date=8 November 2018|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20181108025856/https://www.uefa.com/uefanationsleague/season=2019/standings/#/ld|url-status=dead}}</ref> Their first Nations League match saw [[Armenia national football team|Armenia]] beat them 2–1 away. Liechtenstein were able to claim their first Nations League victory, beating [[Gibraltar national football team|Gibraltar]] 2–0 at home.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.uefa.com/uefanationsleague/teams/81--liechtenstein/|title=Liechtenstein - UEFA Nations League|website=[[UEFA]]|access-date=27 January 2023|archive-date=27 January 2023|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20230127053604/https://www.uefa.com/uefanationsleague/teams/81--liechtenstein/|url-status=live}}</ref> Liechtenstein finished bottom of their group with just one win and a draw, also at home, against Armenia (2–2).

During the [[UEFA Euro 2020 qualifying Group J|UEFA Euro 2020 qualifiers]], Liechtenstein managed to pick up two points thanks to two draws, one away to [[Greece national football team|Greece]] (1–1) and the other at home to Armenia (1–1), but finished bottom of their group with 2 goals scored and 31 conceded.

In the [[2020–21 UEFA Nations League D#Group 2|2020–21 edition of the Nations League]], Liechtenstein failed to gain promotion to League C, with a single win (2–0 at [[San Marino national football team|San Marino]]), two draws (0–0 at home to [[San Marino national football team|San Marino]] and 1–1 at [[Gibraltar national football team|Gibraltar]]) and one defeat (0–1 at home to [[Gibraltar national football team|Gibraltar]], their direct rival who eventually gained promotion). The ''Blue-Reds'' disappointed by failing to win a single match at home, once again falling behind [[Gibraltar national football team|Gibraltar]] in the standings as they had done in the previous edition, and being dominated overall in the goalless draw with [[San Marino national football team|San Marino]].

The [[2022 FIFA World Cup qualification – UEFA Group J|2022 FIFA World Cup qualifiers]] once again saw Liechtenstein finish bottom of their group, with just one point from a 1–1 draw away to [[Armenia national football team|Armenia]], and 9 defeats, with 2 goals scored and 34 conceded.

The [[2022–23 UEFA Nations League D|2022–23 edition of the UEFA Nations League]] was also a disappointment for Liechtenstein, who finished bottom of their group with 6 defeats in as many games played and just one goal scored, away against [[Andorra national football team|Andorra]] (1–2), their worst record in this competition in 3 editions. 

Liechtenstein began 2024 with four friendly matches, losing two and drawing two. One of the draws came away from home against [[Romania national football team|Romania]] a few days prior to [[UEFA Euro 2024]], a tournament which Romania had qualified for undefeated.

Liechtenstein began their [[2024–25 UEFA Nations League D|2024–25 Nations League]] campaign with a 0–1 loss away to San Marino after a Liechtenstein goal was denied through offside, handing the Sammarinese their first competitive victory.<ref name="SMR"/> Following this, Liechtenstein proceeded to draw Gibraltar twice (2–2 away and 0–0 at home with a decisive penalty missed in the stoppage time during the return match against Gibraltar) before losing to San Marino again, this time 1–3 in Vaduz, finishing bottom of Group D1 on 2 points.<ref>{{cite news|url= https://www.bbc.com/sport/football/live/c624w896kd4t|title=Liechtenstein 1-3 San Marino: Visitors earn promotion with first-ever away win|publisher=[[British Broadcasting Corporation]]|date=18 November 2024}}</ref> Between the two draws with Gibraltar, Liechtenstein ended a 41-game winless streak with a 1–0 victory over [[Hong Kong national football team|Hong Kong]].<ref>{{cite news|url= https://www.scmp.com/sport/football/article/3281932/hong-kong-beaten-liechtenstein-who-snap-international-footballs-longest-winless-run|title= Hong Kong coach slams Liechtenstein for 'time wasting', behaving like they 'won World Cup'|publisher=[[South China Morning Post]]|date=11 October 2024}}</ref>

Liechtenstein then went on a 12-match losing streak spanning from the end of 2025 throughout 2025. On 26 March 2026, at the [[2026 FIFA Series (men's matches)|2026 FIFA Series]], Liechtenstein ended the streak with a 1–0 upset victory against [[Tanzania national football team|Tanzania]], who were ranked 110th in the FIFA Rankings, 95 places higher than Liechtenstein. <ref>{{cite web|url=https://tanzaniainsight.com/tanzania-slips-three-places-in-latest-fifa-rankings/|title=Tanzania slips three places in latest FIFA rankings|website=Tanzania Insight|date=28 March 2026}}</ref>

==Results and fixtures==
{{Main|Liechtenstein national football team results (2020–present)}}
The following is a list of match results in the last 12 months, as well as any future matches that have been scheduled.

{{legend2|#CCFFCC|Win|border=1px solid #AAAAAA}}
{{legend2|#FFFFCC|Draw|border=1px solid #AAAAAA}}
{{legend2|#FFCCCC|Loss|border=1px solid #AAAAAA}}
{{legend2|#FFFFFF|Fixture|border=1px solid #AAAAAA}}

===2025===
{{football box collapsible|format=1
|date              = 4 September 2025
|time              = {{UTZ|20:45|2}}
|round             = [[2026 FIFA World Cup qualification – UEFA Group J|2026 FIFA World Cup qualification]]
|team1             = {{fb-rt|LIE}}
|score             = 0–6
|report            = https://www.uefa.com/european-qualifiers/match/2044215/
|team2             = {{fb|BEL}}
|goals1            =
|goals2            = *[[Maxim De Cuyper|De Cuyper]] {{goal|29}}
*[[Youri Tielemans|Tielemans]] {{goal|46||70|pen.}}
*[[Arthur Theate|Theate]] {{goal|60}}
*[[Kevin De Bruyne|De Bruyne]] {{goal|62}}
*[[Malick Fofana|Fofana]] {{goal|90+1}}
|stadium           = [[Rheinpark Stadion]]
|location          = [[Vaduz]], Liechtenstein 
|attendance        = 3,158
|referee           = Morten Krogh ([[Danish Football Association|Denmark]])
|result            = L
}}
{{football box collapsible|format=1
|date              = 7 September 2025
|time              = {{UTZ|18:00|2}}
|round             = [[2026 FIFA World Cup qualification – UEFA Group J|2026 FIFA World Cup qualification]]
|team1             = {{fb-rt|MKD}}
|score             = 5–0
|report            = https://www.uefa.com/european-qualifiers/match/2044236/
|team2             = {{fb|LIE}}
|goals1            =
*[[Benjamin Büchel|B. Büchel]] {{goal|15|o.g.}}
*[[Enis Bardhi|Bardhi]] {{goal|52}}
*[[Darko Churlinov|Churlinov]] {{goal|56}}
*[[Lirim Qamili|Qamili]] {{goal|82}}
*[[Luka Stankovski|Stankovski]] {{goal|90}}
|goals2            =
|stadium           = [[Toše Proeski Arena]]
|location          = [[Skopje]], North Macedonia
|attendance        = 8,693
|referee           = Horațiu Feșnic ([[Romanian Football Federation|Romania]])
|result            = L
}}
{{football box collapsible|format=1
|date              = 10 October 2025
|time              = {{UTZ|19:00|5}}
|round             = [[2026 FIFA World Cup qualification – UEFA Group J|2026 FIFA World Cup qualification]]
|team1             = {{fb-rt|KAZ}}
|score             = 4–0
|report            = https://www.uefa.com/european-qualifiers/match/2044265/
|team2             = {{fb|LIE}}
|goals1            = *[[Galymzhan Kenzhebek|Kenzhebek]] {{goal|26||59}}
*[[Bakhtiyar Zaynutdinov|Zaynutdinov]] {{goal|28}}
*[[Alibek Kasym|Kasym]] {{goal|81}}
|goals2            =
|stadium           = [[Astana Arena]]
|location          = [[Astana]], Kazakhstan
|attendance        = 26,539
|referee           = [[Paweł Raczkowski]] ([[Polish Football Association|Poland]])
|result            = L
}}
{{football box collapsible|format=1
|date              = 13 October 2025
|time              = {{UTZ|18:00|+2}}
|round             = [[Exhibition game#Association football|Friendly]]
|team1             = {{fb-rt|MNE}}
|score             = 2–1
|report            = https://www.uefa.com/european-qualifiers/friendlies/match/2045701--montenegro-vs-liechtenstein/
|team2             = {{fb|LIE}}
|goals1            =
*[[Milutin Osmajić|Osmajić]] {{goal|74}}
*[[Viktor Đukanović|Đukanović]] {{goal|88}}
|goals2            =
*[[Aron Sele|Sele]] {{goal|27}}
|stadium           = [[Podgorica City Stadium]]
|location          = [[Podgorica]], Montenegro
|attendance        = 1,732
|referee           = [[Igor Stojchevski]] ([[Football Federation of Macedonia|North Macedonia]])
|result            = L
}}
{{football box collapsible|format=1
|date              = 15 November 2025
|time              = {{UTZ|18:00|1}}
|round             = [[2026 FIFA World Cup qualification – UEFA Group J|2026 FIFA World Cup qualification]]
|team1             = {{fb-rt|LIE}}
|score             = 0–1
|report            = https://www.uefa.com/european-qualifiers/match/2044328/
|team2             = {{fb|WAL}}
|goals1            =
|goals2            =
*[[Jordan James (footballer, born 2004)|J. James]] {{goal|61}}
|stadium           = [[Rheinpark Stadion]]
|location          = [[Vaduz]], Liechtenstein 
|attendance        =  5,563
|referee           = Juxhin Xhaja ([[Albanian Football Federation|Albania]])
|result            = L
}}
{{football box collapsible|format=1
|date              = 18 November 2025
|time              = {{UTZ|20:45|1}}
|round             = [[2026 FIFA World Cup qualification – UEFA Group J|2026 FIFA World Cup qualification]]
|team1             = {{fb-rt|BEL}}
|score             = 7–0
|report            = https://www.uefa.com/european-qualifiers/match/2044352/
|team2             = {{fb|LIE}}
|goals1            =
*[[Hans Vanaken|Vanaken]] {{goal|3}}
*[[Jérémy Doku|Doku]] {{goal|34||41}}
*[[Brandon Mechele|Mechele]] {{goal|52}}
*[[Alexis Saelemaekers|Saelemaekers]] {{goal|55}}
*[[Charles De Ketelaere|De Ketelaere]] {{goal|57||59}}
|goals2            =
|stadium           = [[Stade Maurice Dufrasne]]
|location          = [[Liège]], Belgium
|attendance        = 26,253
|referee           = [[Mohammed Al-Hakim]] ([[Swedish Football Association|Sweden]])
|result            = L
}}

===2026===
{{football box collapsible|format=1
|round      = [[2026 FIFA Series (men's matches)|2026 FIFA Series]]
|date       = 26 March 2026
|time       = {{UTZ|16:30|+2}}
|team1      = {{fb-rt|TAN}}
|score      = 0–1
|report     = https://www.fifa.com/en/match-centre/match/10017/292887/292888/400021870?date=2026-03-26
|team2      = {{fb|LIE}}
|goals1     = 
|goals2     = [[Ferhat Sağlam|Sağlam]] {{goal|55}}
|location   = [[Kigali]], Rwanda
|stadium    = [[Kigali Pelé Stadium]]
|attendance = 4,500
|referee    = Mohamed Diraneh ([[Djiboutian Football Federation|Djibouti]])
|result     = W
}}
{{football box collapsible|format=1
|round      = [[2026 FIFA Series (men's matches)|2026 FIFA Series]]
|date       = 29 March 2026
|time       = {{UTZ|18:00|+2}}
|team1      = {{fb-rt|ARU}}
|score      = 4–1 
|report     = https://www.fifa.com/it/match-centre/match/10017/292887/292888/400021873 
|team2      = {{fb|LIE}}
|goals1     =
*[[Jaybrien Romano|Romano]] {{goal|18}}
*[[Gello Robertha|Robertha]] {{goal|25}}
*[[Rovien Ostiana|Ostiana]] {{sentoff|2|48|61}}
*[[Rainey Breinburg|Breinburg]] {{goal|69}}
*[[Jens Hofer|Hofer]] {{goal|90+4|o.g.}}
|goals2     =
*[[Emanuel Zünd|Zünd]] {{goal|52}} 
|location   = [[Kigali]], Rwanda
|stadium    = [[Kigali Pelé Stadium]]
|attendance = 
|referee    = Samuel Uwikunda ([[Rwanda Football Federation|Rwanda]])
|result     = L 
}}
{{football box collapsible|format=1
|date = 4 June 2026
|time = {{UTZ|19:00|+2}}
|round = [[Exhibition game#Association football|Friendly]]
|team1 = {{fb-rt|AND}}
|score = 2–0
|report = https://www.uefa.com/european-qualifiers/friendlies/match/2048304--andorra-vs-liechtenstein/
|team2 = {{fb|LIE}}
|goals1 = 
*[[Àlex Martínez (footballer, born 1998)|Martínez]] {{goal|74}}
*[[Jordi Aláez|Aláez]] {{goal|81|pen.}}
|goals2 = 
|stadium = [[Estadi de la FAF]]
|location = [[Encamp]], Andorra
|attendance  = 1,000
|referee = Javier Alberola ([[Royal Spanish Football Federation|Spain]])
|result = L
}}
{{football box collapsible|format=1
|date = 7 June 2026
|time = 15:00 ([[UTC+02:00|UTC+2]])
|round = [[Exhibition game#Association football|Friendly]]
|team1 = {{fb-rt|LIE}}
|score = 0–2
|report = https://www.uefa.com/european-qualifiers/friendlies/match/2048307--liechtenstein-vs-cyprus/
|team2 = {{fb|CYP}}
|goals1 =
|goals2 = 
*[[Marinos Tzionis|Tzionis]] {{goal|28}}
*[[Konstantinos Sotiriou|Sotiriou]] {{goal|70}}
|stadium = [[Rheinpark Stadion]]
|location = [[Vaduz]], Liechtenstein
|attendance = 878 
|referee = Anojen Kanagasingam ([[Swiss Football Association|Switzerland]])
|result = L
}}
{{football box collapsible
|format     = 1
|date       = 24 September 2026
|time       = {{UTZ|20:45|+2}}
|round      = [[2026–27 UEFA Nations League D|2026–27 UEFA Nations League]]
|score      = 
|report     = https://www.uefa.com/uefanationsleague/match/2048008/
|team1      = {{fb-rt|LIE}}
|goals1     = 
|team2      = {{fb|LTU}}
|goals2     = 
|stadium    = [[Rheinpark Stadion]]
|location   = [[Vaduz]], Liechtenstein
|attendance = 
|referee    = 
|result     = 
}}
{{football box collapsible
|format     = 1
|date       = 1 October 2026
|time       = {{UTZ|20:00|+4}}
|round      = [[2026–27 UEFA Nations League D|2026–27 UEFA Nations League]]
|score      = 
|report     = https://www.uefa.com/uefanationsleague/match/2047955/
|team1      = {{fb-rt|AZE}}
|goals1     = 
|team2      = {{fb|LIE}}
|goals2     = 
|stadium    = [[Tofiq Bahramov Republican Stadium]]
|location   = [[Baku]], Azerbaijan
|attendance = 
|referee    = 
|result     = 
}}
{{football box collapsible
|format     = 1
|date       = 13 November 2026
|time       = {{UTZ|20:45|+1}}
|round      = [[2026–27 UEFA Nations League D|2026–27 UEFA Nations League]]
|score      = 
|report     = https://www.uefa.com/uefanationsleague/match/2047885/
|team1      = {{fb-rt|LIE}}
|goals1     = 
|team2      = {{fb|AZE}}
|goals2     = 
|stadium    = [[Rheinpark Stadion]]
|location   = [[Vaduz]], Liechtenstein
|attendance = 
|referee    = 
|result     = 
}}
{{football box collapsible
|format     = 1
|date       = 16 November 2026
|time       = {{UTZ|19:00|+2}}
|round      = [[2026–27 UEFA Nations League D|2026–27 UEFA Nations League]]
|score      = 
|report     = https://www.uefa.com/uefanationsleague/match/2047911/
|team1      = {{fb-rt|LTU}}
|goals1     = 
|team2      = {{fb|LIE}}
|goals2     = 
|stadium    = [[Darius and Girėnas Stadium]]
|location   = [[Kaunas]], Lithuania
|attendance = 
|referee    = 
|result     = 
}}

==Manager history==
[[File:Martin Stocklasa - SV Ried (03).jpg|thumb|[[Martin Stocklasa]], the team manager from 2020 to 2023.]]
{{Div col|colwidth=17em}}
* {{flagicon|Liechtenstein}} [[Erich Bürzle]] (1990)
* {{flagicon|Germany}} [[Dietrich Weise]] (1990–1996)
* {{flagicon|Austria}} [[Alfred Riedl]] (1997–1998)
* {{flagicon|Liechtenstein}} [[Erich Bürzle]] (1998)
* {{flagicon|Germany}} [[Ralf Loose]] (1998–2003)
* {{flagicon|Austria}} [[Walter Hörmann]] (2003–2004)
* {{flagicon|Switzerland}} [[Martin Andermatt]] (2004–2006)
* {{flagicon|Switzerland}} [[Urs Meier (footballer)|Urs Meier]] (2006)
* {{flagicon|Switzerland}} [[Hans-Peter Zaugg]] (2006–2012)
* {{flagicon|Austria}} [[Rene Pauritsch]] (2013–2018)
* {{flagicon|Iceland}} [[Helgi Kolviðsson]] (2018–2020)
* {{flagicon|Liechtenstein}} [[Martin Stocklasa]] (2020–2023)
* {{Flagicon|Austria}} [[Rene Pauritsch]] (2023)
* {{Flagicon|Germany}} [[Konrad Fünfstück]] (2023–)
* {{div col end}}

==Players==

===Current squad===
The following players were called up for the [[Exhibition match|friendly]] matches against [[Andorra national football team|Andorra]] and [[Cyprus national football team|Cyprus]] on 4 and 7 June 2026, respectively.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.lfv.li/fileadmin/user_upload/Dateien/Nationalmannschaften/A-Nationalmannschaft/Aufgebote-Nationalmannschaft/2026/20260528_Kader.pdf|title=Kader Liechtensteiner Fussballverband |website=LFV News Instagram|language=de|date=28 May 2026}}</ref>

''Caps and goals are current as of 7 June 2026, after the match against [[Cyprus national football team|Cyprus]].''

{{nat fs g start}}
{{nat fs g player|no=|pos=GK|name=[[Justin Ospelt]]|age={{Birth date and age|1999|9|7|df=yes}}|caps=10|goals=0|club=[[Sportfreunde Siegen|SF Siegen]]|clubnat=GER}}
{{nat fs g player|no=|pos=GK|name=[[Gabriel Foser]]|age={{birth date and age|2002|9|2|df=yes}}|caps=0|goals=0|club=[[USV Eschen/Mauren|Eschen/Mauren]]|clubnat=LIE}}
{{nat fs g player|no=|pos=GK|name=[[Lorenzo Lo Russo]]|age={{birth date and age|1993|7|8|df=yes}}|caps=0|goals=0|club=[[FC Balzers|Balzers]]|clubnat=LIE}}
{{nat fs g player|no=|pos=GK|name=[[Silvan Schädler]]|age={{birth date and age|2006|2|6|df=yes}}|caps=0|goals=0|club=[[FC Vaduz|Vaduz II]]|clubnat=LIE}}
{{nat fs break}}
{{nat fs g player|no=|pos=DF|name=[[Maximilian Göppel]]|age={{birth date and age|1997|8|31|df=yes}}|caps=82|goals=2|club=[[FC Freienbach|Freienbach]]|clubnat=SUI}}
{{nat fs g player|no=|pos=DF|name=[[Livio Meier]]|age={{birth date and age|1998|1|10|df=yes}}|caps=61|goals=1|club=[[USV Eschen/Mauren|Eschen/Mauren]]|clubnat=LIE}}
{{nat fs g player|no=|pos=DF|name=[[Andreas Malin]]|age={{birth date and age|1994|1|31|df=yes}}|caps=57|goals=0|club=[[SC Göfis|Göfis]]|clubnat=AUT}}
{{nat fs g player|no=|pos=DF|name=[[Jens Hofer]]|age={{birth date and age|1997|10|1|df=yes}}|caps=46|goals=0|club=[[SC Düdingen|Düdingen]]|clubnat=SUI}}
{{nat fs g player|no=|pos=DF|name=[[Niklas Beck]]|age={{birth date and age|2001|3|25|df=yes}}|caps=27|goals=0|club=[[FC Balzers|Balzers]]|clubnat=LIE}}
{{nat fs g player|no=|pos=DF|name=[[Felix Oberwaditzer]]|age={{birth date and age|2006|3|14|df=yes}}|caps=7|goals=0|club=[[SC Austria Lustenau|Austria Lustenau]]|clubnat=AUT}}
{{nat fs g player|no=|pos=DF|name=[[Jonas Weissenhofer]]|age={{birth date and age|2006|7|25|df=yes}}|caps=7|goals=0|club=[[VfB Hohenems]]|clubnat=AUT}}
{{nat fs break}}
{{nat fs g player|no=|pos=MF|name=[[Sandro Wolfinger]]|age={{Birth date and age|1991|8|24|df=yes}}|caps=79|goals=3|club=[[FC Balzers|Balzers]]|clubnat=LIE}}
{{nat fs g player|no=|pos=MF|name=[[Aron Sele]]|age={{birth date and age|1996|9|2|df=yes}}|caps=76|goals=2|club=[[SC Young Fellows Juventus|YF Juventus]]|clubnat=SUI}}
{{nat fs g player|no=|pos=MF|name=[[Marcel Büchel]]|age={{birth date and age|1991|3|18|df=yes}}|caps=31|goals=1|club=[[UniPomezia 1938|Pomezia]]|clubnat=ITA}}
{{nat fs g player|no=|pos=MF|name=[[Lars Traber]]|age={{birth date and age|2000|6|12|df=yes}}|caps=28|goals=0|club=[[SC Brühl|Brühl]]|clubnat=SUI}}
{{nat fs g player|no=|pos=MF|name=[[Andrin Netzer]]|age={{birth date and age|2002|1|11|df=yes}}|caps=23|goals=0|club=[[FC Dornbirn 1913|Dornbirn]]|clubnat=AUT}}
{{nat fs g player|no=|pos=MF|name=[[Kenny Kindle]]|age={{Birth date and age|2003|11|29|df=y}}|caps=19|goals=0|club=[[FC Balzers|Balzers]]|clubnat=LIE}}
{{nat fs g player|no=|pos=MF|name=[[Emanuel Zünd]]|age={{birth date and age|2004|12|29|df=yes}}|caps=16|goals=1|club=[[FC Meyrin|Meyrin]]|clubnat=SUI}}
{{nat fs g player|no=|pos=MF|name=[[Severin Schlegel]]|age={{birth date and age|2004|7|24|df=yes}}|caps=13|goals=0|club=[[FC Balzers|Balzers]]|clubnat=LIE}}
{{nat fs g player|no=|pos=MF|name=[[Alessio Hasler]]|age={{birth date and age|2005|7|7|df=yes}}|caps=12|goals=0|club=[[FC Vaduz|Vaduz]]|clubnat=LIE}}
{{nat fs g player|no=|pos=MF|name=[[Liam Kranz]]|age={{birth date and age|2003|7|17|df=yes}}|caps=12|goals=0|club=[[FC Schaan|Schaan]]|clubnat=LIE}}
{{nat fs g player|no=|pos=MF|name=[[Luca Beck]]|age={{birth date and age|2003|7|12|df=yes}}|caps=1|goals=0|club=[[FC Widnau|Widnau]]|clubnat=SUI}}
{{nat fs break}}
{{nat fs g player|no=|pos=FW|name=[[Dennis Salanović]]|age={{birth date and age|1996|2|26|df=yes}}|caps=67|goals=4|club=[[FC Linth 04|Linth 04]]|clubnat=SUI}}
{{nat fs g player|no=|pos=FW|name=[[Ferhat Sağlam]]|age={{birth date and age|2001|10|10|df=yes}}|caps=26|goals=2|club=[[SV Eintracht Hohkeppel|Eintracht Hohkeppel]]|clubnat=GER}}
{{nat fs g player|no=|pos=FW|name=[[Fabio Luque-Notaro]]|age={{Birth date and age|2005|8|31|df=y}}|caps=25|goals=0|club=[[1. FC Schweinfurt 05|Schweinfurt 05]]|clubnat=GER}}
{{nat fs g player|no=|pos=FW|name=[[Willy Pizzi]]|age={{Birth date and age|1994|12|28|df=y}}|caps=10|goals=0|club=[[USV Eschen/Mauren|Eschen/Mauren]]|clubnat=LIE}}
{{nat fs g player|no=|pos=FW|name=[[Philipp Gaßner]]|age={{Birth date and age|2003|8|30|df=y}}|caps=7|goals=0|club=[[VfB Hohenems|Hohenems]]|clubnat=AUT}}
{{nat fs g player|no=|pos=FW|name=[[Julien Hasler]]|age={{Birth date and age|1989|9|22|df=y}}|caps=7|goals=0|club=[[FC Schaan|Schaan]]|clubnat=LIE}}
{{nat fs g player|no=|pos=FW|name=[[Bruno Poitner]]|age={{Birth date and age|2007|3|22|df=y}}|caps=2|goals=0|club=[[USV Eschen/Mauren|Eschen/Mauren]]|clubnat=LIE}}
{{nat fs end}}

===Recent call-ups===
The following players were called up in the last 12 months and are still eligible to represent.
<!--Sorted by position, most recent call-up, caps, goals.-->
{{nat fs r start}}
{{nat fs r player|no=|pos=GK|name=[[Benjamin Büchel]]|other=[[Captain (association football)|vice-captain]]|age={{Birth date and age|1989|7|4|df=yes}}|caps=82|goals=0|club=[[FC Vaduz|Vaduz]]|clubnat=LIE|latest=v. {{fb|ARU}}, 29 March 2026}}
{{nat fs r player|no=|pos=GK|name=[[Tim-Tiado Oehri]]|age={{Birth date and age|2003|12|15|df=yes}}|caps=0|goals=0|club=[[FC Vaduz|Vaduz]]|clubnat=LIE|latest=v. {{fb|WAL}}, 15 November 2025}}
{{nat fs break}}
{{nat fs r player|no=|pos=DF|name=[[Lukas Graber]]|age={{birth date and age|2001|5|3|df=yes}}|caps=7|goals=0|club=[[USV Eschen/Mauren|Eschen/Mauren]]|clubnat=LIE|latest=v. {{fb|MNE}}, 13 October 2025}}
{{nat fs break}}
{{nat fs r player|no=|pos=MF|name=[[Nicolas Hasler]]|other=[[Captain (association football)|captain]]|age={{Birth date and age|1991|5|4|df=yes}}|caps=111|goals=7|club=[[FC Vaduz|Vaduz]]|clubnat=LIE|latest=v. {{fb|ARU}}, 29 March 2026}}
{{nat fs r player|no=|pos=MF|name=[[Fabio Wolfinger]]|age={{Birth date and age|1996|11|5|df=yes}}|caps=37|goals=1|club=[[FC Balzers|Balzers]]|clubnat=LIE|latest=v. {{fb|ARU}}, 29 March 2026}}
{{nat fs r player|no=|pos=MF|name=[[Simon Lüchinger]]|age={{birth date and age|2002|11|28|df=yes}}|caps=36|goals=0|club=[[Schwarz-Weiß Bregenz|SW Bregenz]]|clubnat=AUT|latest=v. {{fb|BEL}}, 18 November 2025}}
{{nat fs r player|no=|pos=MF|name=[[Jakob Lorenz]]|age={{birth date and age|2001|9|11|df=yes}}|caps=4|goals=0|club=[[FC Blau-Weiß feldkirk|Blau-Weiß Feldkirch]]|clubnat=AUT|latest=v. {{fb|MNE}}, 13 October 2025}}
{{nat fs r player|no=|pos=MF|name=[[Colin Haas]]|age={{birth date and age|1996|5|30|df=yes}}|caps=2|goals=0|club=[[FC Ruggell|Ruggell]]|clubnat=LIE|latest=v. {{fb|MNE}}, 13 October 2025}}
{{nat fs break}}
{{nat fs r player|no=|pos=FW|name=[[Agim Zeciri]]|age={{Birth date and age|1984|3|16|df=y}}|caps=0|goals=0|club=[[FC Ruggell|Ruggell]]|clubnat=LIE|latest=v. {{fb|ARU}}, 29 March 2026}}
{{nat fs r player|no=|pos=FW|name=[[Philipp Ospelt]]|age={{birth date and age|1992|10|7|df=yes}}|caps=23|goals=0|club=[[FC Ruggell|Ruggell]]|clubnat=LIE|latest=v. {{fb|MNE}}, 13 October 2025}}
{{nat fs end}}

==Player records==
:{{updated|7 June 2026}}<ref>{{cite web |last1=Garin |first1=Erik |title=Liechtenstein - Record International Players |url=https://www.rsssf.org/miscellaneous/lie-recintlp.html |website=RSSSF |access-date=2 February 2023 |archive-date=6 December 2022 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20221206142010/https://www.rsssf.org/miscellaneous/lie-recintlp.html |url-status=live }}</ref>
:''Players in '''bold''' are still active with Liechtenstein.''

===Most appearances===
[[File:Peter Jehle.jpg|220px|thumb|right|Peter Jehle is Liechtenstein's most capped player at 132 caps.]]
{| class="wikitable sortable" style="text-align: center;"
|-
!width=25|Rank
!width=150|Player
!width=50|Caps
!width=50|Goals
!width=100|Career
|-
|1
|align=left| [[Peter Jehle]]
|132
|0
| 1998–2018
|-
|2
|align=left| [[Mario Frick (footballer)|Mario Frick]]
|125
|16
| 1993–2015
|-
|3
|align=left| [[Martin Stocklasa]]
|113
|5
| 1996–2014
|-
|4
|align=left| [[Franz Burgmeier]]
|112
|9
| 2001–2018
|-
|5
|align=left| '''[[Nicolas Hasler]]'''
|111
|7
| 2010–present
|-
|6
|align=left| [[Thomas Beck (footballer)|Thomas Beck]]
|92
|5
| 1998–2013
|-
|7
|align=left| [[Martin Büchel]]
|91
|2
| 2004–2021
|-
|rowspan="2"|8
|align=left| '''[[Benjamin Büchel]]'''
|82
|0
| 2008–present
|-
|align=left| '''[[Maximilian Göppel]]'''
|82
|2
| 2016–present
|-
|rowspan="2"|10
|align=left| [[Michele Polverino]]
|79
|6
| 2007–2019
|-
|align=left| [[Sandro Wolfinger]]
|79
|3
| 2013–2026
|-
|}

===Top goalscorers===
[[File:Mario Frick (footballer).jpg|215px|thumb|right|Mario Frick is Liechtenstein's all-time record goalscorer at 16 goals.]]
{| class="wikitable sortable" style="text-align:center"
|-
!width=25px|Rank
!width=150px|Player
!width=50px|Goals
!width=50px|Caps
!width=50px|Ratio
!width=100px|Career
|-
|1
|align=left| [[Mario Frick (footballer)|Mario Frick]]
|16
|125
| {{#expr:16/125 round 2}}
| 1993–2015
|-
|2
|align=left| [[Franz Burgmeier]]
|9
|112
| {{#expr:9/112 round 2}}
| 2001–2018
|-
|3
|align=left| '''[[Nicolas Hasler]]'''
|7
|111
| {{#expr:7/111 round 2}}
| 2010–present
|-
|4
|align=left| [[Michele Polverino]]
|6
|79
| {{#expr:6/79 round 2}}
| 2007–2019
|-
| rowspan="2"|5
|align=left| [[Thomas Beck (footballer)|Thomas Beck]]
|5
|92
| {{#expr:5/92 round 2}}
| 1998–2013
|-
|align=left| [[Martin Stocklasa]]
|5
|113
| {{#expr:5/113 round 2}}
| 1996–2014
|-
|7
|align=left| '''[[Dennis Salanović]]'''
|4
|67
| {{#expr:4/67 round 2}}
| 2014–present
|-
|rowspan="2"|8
|align=left| [[Yanik Frick]]
|3
|30
| {{#expr:3/30 round 2}}
| 2016–2022
|-
|align=left| [[Sandro Wolfinger]]
|3
|79
| {{#expr:3/79 round 2}}
| 2013–2026
|-
| rowspan="11"|10
|-
|align=left| [[Noah Frick]]
|2
|20
| {{#expr:2/20 round 2}}
| 2019–2023
|-
|-
|align=left| [[Benjamin Fischer (footballer)|Benjamin Fischer]]
|2
|23
| {{#expr:2/23 round 2}}
| 2005–2011
|-
|align=left| '''[[Ferhat Sağlam]]'''
|2
|26
| {{#expr:2/26 round 2}}
| 2023–present
|-
|align=left| [[Mathias Christen]]
|2
|36
| {{#expr:2/36 round 2}}
| 2008–2014
|-
|align=left| [[Fabio D'Elia]]
|2
|50
| {{#expr:2/50 round 2}}
| 2001–2010
|-
|align=left| '''[[Sandro Wieser]]'''
|2
|69
| {{#expr:2/69 round 2}}
| 2008–present
|-
|align=left| [[Michael Stocklasa]]
|2
|71
| {{#expr:2/71 round 2}}
| 1998–2012
|-
|align=left| '''[[Aron Sele]]'''
|2
|76
| {{#expr:2/76 round 2}}
| 2016–present
|-
|align=left| '''[[Maximilian Göppel]]'''
|2
|82
| {{#expr:2/82 round 2}}
| 2016–present
|-
|align=left| [[Martin Büchel]]
|2
|91
| {{#expr:2/91 round 2}}
| 2004–2021
|-
|}

==Competitive record==

===FIFA World Cup===
{| class="wikitable sortable" style="text-align: center;"
|-
!colspan=8|[[FIFA World Cup]] record
|rowspan=15|
!colspan=7|[[FIFA World Cup qualification|Qualification]] record
|-
!Year
!Result
!{{Tooltip|Pld|Games played}}
!{{Tooltip|W|Won}}
!{{Tooltip|D*|Drawn}}
!{{Tooltip|L|Lost}}
!{{Tooltip|GF|Goals for}}
!{{Tooltip|GA|Goals against}}
!Result
!Pld
!W
!D*
!L
!GF
!GA
|-
|[[1930 FIFA World Cup|1930]] to [[1974 FIFA World Cup|1974]]
|colspan=7|''Not a FIFA member''
|colspan=7|''Not a FIFA member''
|-
|[[1978 FIFA World Cup|1978]] to [[1994 FIFA World Cup|1994]]
|colspan=7|''Did not enter''
|colspan=7|''Did not enter''
|-
|{{flagicon|France|1974}} [[1998 FIFA World Cup|1998]]
|colspan=7 rowspan=8|''Did not qualify''
|6/6||10||0||0|||10||3||52
|-
|{{flagicon|South Korea|1997}} {{flagicon|Japan}} [[2002 FIFA World Cup|2002]]
|5/5||8||0||0||8||0||23
|-
|{{flagicon|Germany}} [[2006 FIFA World Cup|2006]]
|6/7||12||2||2||8||13||23
|-
|{{flagicon|South Africa}} [[2010 FIFA World Cup|2010]]
|6/6||10||0||2||8||2||23
|-
|{{flagicon|Brazil}} [[2014 FIFA World Cup|2014]]
||6/6||10||0||2||8||4||25
|-
|{{flagicon|Russia}} [[2018 FIFA World Cup|2018]]
|6/6||10||0||0||10||1||39
|-
|{{flagicon|Qatar}} [[2022 FIFA World Cup|2022]]
|6/6||10||0||1||9||2||34
|-
|{{flagicon|Canada}} {{flagicon|Mexico}} {{flagicon|United States}} [[2026 FIFA World Cup|2026]]
|5/5||8||0||0||8||0||31
|-
|{{flagicon|Morocco}} {{flagicon|Portugal}} {{flagicon|Spain}} [[2030 FIFA World Cup|2030]]
|colspan=7 rowspan=2|''To be determined''
|colspan=7 rowspan=2|''To be determined''
|-
|{{flagicon|Saudi Arabia}} [[2034 FIFA World Cup|2034]]
|-
!Total||{{Tooltip|0/13|Number of tournaments qualified for}}||–||–||–||–||–||–
!—||78||2||7||69||25||250
|}
:''*Draws include knockout matches decided via [[Penalty shoot-out (association football)|penalty shoot-out]].''

===UEFA European Championship===
{| class="wikitable sortable" style="text-align: center;"
!colspan=8|[[UEFA European Championship]] record
|rowspan=15|
!colspan=7|[[UEFA European Championship qualifying|Qualifying]] record
|-
!Year
!Result
!{{Tooltip|Pld|Games played}}
!{{Tooltip|W|Won}}
!{{Tooltip|D*|Drawn}}
!{{Tooltip|L|Lost}}
!{{Tooltip|GF|Goals for}}
!{{Tooltip|GA|Goals against}}
!Result
!Pld
!W
!D*
!L
!GF
!GA
|-
|[[1960 European Nations' Cup|1960]] to [[UEFA Euro 1972|1972]]
|colspan=7|''Not a UEFA member'' 
|colspan=7|''Not a UEFA member''
|-
|[[UEFA Euro 1976|1976]] to [[UEFA Euro 1992|1992]]
|colspan=7|''Did not enter'' 
|colspan=7|''Did not enter''
|-
|{{flagicon|England}} [[UEFA Euro 1996|1996]]
|colspan=7 rowspan=8|''Did not qualify''
|6/6||10||0||1||9||1||40
|-
|{{flagicon|Belgium}} {{flagicon|Netherlands}} [[UEFA Euro 2000|2000]]
|6/6||10||1||1||8||2||39
|-
|{{flagicon|Portugal}} [[UEFA Euro 2004|2004]]
|5/5||8||0||1||7||2||22
|-
|{{flagicon|Austria}} {{flagicon|Switzerland}} [[UEFA Euro 2008|2008]]
|7/7||12||2||1||9||9||32
|-
|{{flagicon|Poland}} {{flagicon|Ukraine}} [[UEFA Euro 2012|2012]]
|5/5||8||1||1||6||3||17
|-
|{{flagicon|France|1974}} [[UEFA Euro 2016|2016]]
|5/6||10||1||2||7||2||26
|-
|{{flagicon|European Union}} [[UEFA Euro 2020|2020]]
|6/6||10|||0||2||8||2||31
|-
|{{flagicon|Germany}} [[UEFA Euro 2024|2024]]
|6/6||10||0||0||10||1||28
|-
|{{flagicon|United Kingdom}} {{flagicon|Republic of Ireland}} [[UEFA Euro 2028|2028]]
|colspan=7 rowspan=2|''To be determined''
|colspan=7 rowspan=2|''To be determined''
|-
|{{flagicon|Italy}} {{flagicon|Turkey}} [[UEFA Euro 2032|2032]]
|-
!Total||0/13||–||–||–||–||–||–
!—||78||5||9||64||22||235
|}
:''*Draws include knockout matches decided via penalty shoot-out.''

===UEFA Nations League===
{| class="wikitable sortable" style="text-align:center;"
!colspan=11 |[[UEFA Nations League]] record
|-
!Season
!Division
!Group
!width=28 |{{Tooltip|Pld|Matches played}}
!width=28 |{{Tooltip|W|Won}}
!width=28 |{{Tooltip|D|Drawn}}
!width=28 |{{Tooltip|L|Lost}}
!width=28 |{{Tooltip|GF|Goals for}}
!width=28 |{{Tooltip|GA|Goals against}}
!width=35 |{{Tooltip|P/R|Promotion/relegation at end of season}}
!width=28 |{{Tooltip|RK|Overall rank}}
|-
|[[2018–19 UEFA Nations League|2018–19]]
|[[2018–19 UEFA Nations League D|D]]
|[[2018–19 UEFA Nations League D#Group 4|4]]
|6||1||1||4||7||12|| rowspan="4" |{{same position}}||52nd
|-
|[[2020–21 UEFA Nations League|2020–21]]
|[[2020–21 UEFA Nations League D|D]]
|[[2020–21 UEFA Nations League D#Group 2|2]]
|4||1||2||1||3||2||51st
|-
|[[2022–23 UEFA Nations League|2022–23]]
|[[2022–23 UEFA Nations League D|D]]
|[[2022–23 UEFA Nations League D#Group 1|1]]
|6||0||0||6||1||11||55th
|-
|[[2024–25 UEFA Nations League|2024–25]]
|[[2024–25 UEFA Nations League D|D]]
|[[2024–25 UEFA Nations League D#Group 1|1]]
|4||0||2||2||3||6||53rd
|-
|[[2026–27 UEFA Nations League|2026–27]]
|[[2026–27 UEFA Nations League D|D]]
|[[2026–27 UEFA Nations League D|TBD]]
|colspan=9|''To be determined''
|-
!colspan=3|Total
!20
!2
!5
!13
!14
!31
!colspan=2|51st
|}

==Head-to-head record==
{{updated|7 June 2026}}

{| class="wikitable collapsible collapsed sortable plainrowheaders" style="text-align:center;"
|-
! Against
! Played
! Won
! Drawn
! Lost
! GF
! GA
! GD
|-
!scope=row|{{Fb|ALB}}
|  4|| 0 || 0 || 4 || 0 || 9 || −9
|-
!scope=row|{{Fb|AND}}
|  5|| 1 || 0 || 4 || 2 || 7 || -5
|-
!scope=row|{{Fb|ARM}}
|  6|| 0 || 3 || 3 || 5 || 10 || -5
|-
!scope=row|{{Fb|ARU}}
|  1|| 0 || 0 || 1 || 1 || 4 || −3
|-
!scope=row|{{Fb|AUS}}
|  1|| 0 || 0 || 1 || 1 || 3 || −2
|-
!scope=row|{{Fb|AUT}}
|  9|| 0 || 0 || 9 || 1 || 36|| −35
|-
!scope=row|{{Fb|AZE}}
|  5|| 1 || 1 || 3 || 2 || 8 || −6
|-
!scope=row|{{Fb|BLR}}
|  1|| 0 || 0 || 1 || 1 || 5 || −4
|-
!scope=row|{{Fb|BEL}}
|  2|| 0 || 0 || 2 || 0 || 13 || −13
|-
!scope=row|{{Fb|BIH}}
|  10|| 0 || 1 || 9 || 3 || 35 || −32
|-
!scope=row|{{Fb|CPV}}
|  1|| 0 || 0 || 1 || 0 || 6 || -6
|-
!scope=row|{{Fb|CHN}}
|  1|| 1 || 0 || 0 || 2 || 0 || +2
|-
!scope=row|{{Fb|CRO}}
|  2|| 0 || 0 || 2 || 2 || 8 || −6
|-
!scope=row|{{Fb|CYP}}
|  1|| 0 || 0 || 1 || 0 || 2 || -2
|-
!scope=row|{{Fb|CZE}}
|  2|| 0 || 0 || 2 || 0 || 4 || −4
|-
!scope=row|{{Fb|DEN}}
|  3|| 0 || 0 || 3 || 0 || 13 || −13
|-
!scope=row|{{Fb|ENG}}
|  2|| 0 || 0 || 2 || 0 || 4 || −4
|-
!scope=row|{{Fb|EST}}
|  5|| 0 || 1 || 4 || 2 || 10 || −8
|-
!scope=row|{{Fb|FRO}}
|  8|| 0 || 0 || 8 || 4 || 21 || −17
|-
!scope=row|{{Fb|FIN}}
|  5|| 0 || 2 || 3 || 3 || 9 || −6
|-
!scope=row|{{Fb|GEO}}
|  1|| 0 || 0 || 1 || 0 || 2|| −2
|-
!scope=row|{{Fb|GER}}
|  6|| 0 || 0 || 6 || 3 || 38|| −35
|-
!scope=row|{{Fb|GIB}}
|  8|| 1 || 4 || 3 || 6 || 8 || -2
|-
!scope=row|{{Fb|GRE}}
|  5|| 0 || 1 || 4 || 1 || 8 || −7
|-
!scope=row|{{Fb|HKG}}
|  1|| 1 || 0 || 0 || 1 || 0|| +1
|-
!scope=row|{{Fb|HUN}}
|  3|| 0 || 1 || 2 || 0 || 10|| −10
|-
!scope=row|{{Fb|ISL}}
|  11|| 1 || 2 || 8 || 6 || 35|| −29
|-
!scope=row|{{Fb|INA}}
|  1|| 1 || 0 || 0 || 3 || 2|| +1
|-
!scope=row|{{Fb|ISR}}
|  4|| 0 || 0 || 4 || 1 || 8 || −7
|-
!scope=row|{{Fb|ITA}}
|  4|| 0 || 0 || 4 || 0 || 20 || −20
|-
!scope=row|{{Fb|KAZ}}
|  2|| 0 || 0 || 2 || 0 || 6 || −6
|-
!scope=row|{{Fb|LVA}}
|  12|| 1 || 2 || 9 || 5 || 19|| −14
|-
!scope=row|{{Fb|LIT}}
|  6|| 1 || 1 || 4 || 3 || 8 || −5
|-
!scope=row|{{Fb|LUX}}
|  6|| 3 || 1 || 2 || 12|| 7 || +5
|-
!scope=row|{{Fb|MAS}}
|  1|| 1 || 0 || 0 || 1 || 0|| +1
|-
!scope=row|{{Fb|MLT}}
|  6|| 0 || 1 || 5 || 3 || 16|| −13
|-
!scope=row|{{Fb|MDA}}
|  4|| 1 || 1 || 2 || 2 || 5|| -4
|-
!scope=row|{{Fb|MNE}}
|  3|| 0 || 1 || 2 || 1 || 4|| −3
|-
!scope=row|{{Fb|NED}}
|  1|| 0 || 0 || 1 || 0 || 3 || −3
|-
!scope=row|{{Fb|MKD}}
|  13|| 0 || 1 || 12 || 5 || 50|| −45
|-
!scope=row|{{Fb|NIR}}
|  4|| 0 || 0 || 4 || 6 || 17|| −11
|-
!scope=row|{{Fb|POL}}
|  1|| 0 || 0 || 1 || 0 || 2|| −2
|-
!scope=row|{{Fb|POR}}
|  9|| 0 || 1 || 8 || 3 || 41|| −38
|-
!scope=row|{{Fb|QAT}}
|  1|| 1 || 0 || 0 || 2 || 1 || +1
|-
!scope=row|{{Fb|IRL}}
|  4|| 0 || 1 || 3 || 0 || 14|| −14
|-
!scope=row|{{Fb|ROU}}
|  7|| 0 || 1 || 6 || 1 || 30|| −29
|-
!scope=row|{{Fb|RUS}}
|  5|| 0 || 0 || 5 || 1 || 15 || −14
|-
!scope=row|{{Fb|SMR}}
|  8|| 3 || 2 || 3 || 7 || 7 ||  0
|-
!scope=row|{{Fb|SAU}}
|  1|| 1 || 0 || 0 || 1 || 0 || +1
|-
!scope=row|{{Fb|SCO}}
|  3|| 0 || 0 || 3 || 1 || 7 || −6
|-
!scope=row|{{Fb|SVK}}
|  11|| 0 || 2 || 9 || 1 || 30|| −29
|-
!scope=row|{{Fb|SPA}}
|  8|| 0 || 0 || 8 || 0 || 39|| −39
|-
!scope=row|{{Fb|SWE}}
|  4|| 0 || 0 || 4 || 1 || 10 || −9
|-
!scope=row|{{Fb|SWI}}
|  9|| 0 || 0 || 9 || 1 || 28|| −27
|-
!scope=row|{{Fb|TAN}}
|  1|| 1 || 0 || 0 || 1 || 0|| +1
|-
!scope=row|{{Fb|THA}}
|  1|| 0 || 0 || 1 || 0 || 2|| −2
|-
!scope=row|{{Fb|TOG}}
|  1|| 0 || 0 || 1 || 0 || 1|| −1
|-
!scope=row|{{Fb|TUR}}
|  2|| 0 || 0 || 2 || 0 || 8 || −8
|-
!scope=row|{{Fb|USA}}
|  1|| 0 || 0 || 1 || 1 || 4 || −3
|-
!scope=row|{{Fb|WAL}}
|  5|| 0 || 0 || 5 || 0 || 12 || −12
|- class="sortbottom"
! Total || 254 || 20 || 31 || 206 || 99 || 714 || −615<!-- || {{#expr: 20/253 round 3}}%-->
|}

== In literature ==
Prompted by the team's poor record in competitive games, British writer [[Charlie Connelly]] followed the entire [[2002 FIFA World Cup qualification (UEFA)|qualifying campaign]] for the [[2002 FIFA World Cup]]. As recorded in the subsequent book ''Stamping Grounds: Liechtenstein's Quest for the World Cup'', Liechtenstein lost all eight games without scoring a goal.<ref>{{Cite book|url=https://books.telegraph.co.uk/Product/Charlie-Connelly/Stamping-Grounds--Exploring-Liechtenstein-and-its-World-Cup-Dream/16457289|title=Stamping Grounds : Exploring Liechtenstein and its World Cup Dream|date=2014-06-11|isbn=9780349141121|language=en-gb|last1=Connelly|first1=Charlie|access-date=7 November 2018|archive-date=8 November 2018|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20181108065333/https://books.telegraph.co.uk/Product/Charlie-Connelly/Stamping-Grounds--Exploring-Liechtenstein-and-its-World-Cup-Dream/16457289|url-status=live}}</ref>

==References==
{{Reflist}}

==External links==
{{Commons category}}
* [http://www.lfv.li/ Liechtensteiner Fussballverbund]
* [https://www.uefa.com/nationalassociations/lie/ Liechtenstein] at [[UEFA]]
* [https://www.fifa.com/about-fifa/associations/LIE Liechtenstein] at [[FIFA]]
* [https://www.rsssf.org/miscellaneous/lie-recintlp.html RSSSF archive of most capped players and highest goalscorers]
* [https://web.archive.org/web/20130820110012/http://www.autoren.tv// ''Die Elf''] – documentary film about Liechtenstein national team (archived 20 August 2013)

{{Liechtenstein national football team}}
{{Navboxes
| title = Links to related articles
| bg = #002B7F
| fg = white
| bordercolor = #CE1126
| list =
{{Football in Liechtenstein}}
{{UEFA teams}}
{{National sports teams of Liechtenstein}}
}}
{{Portal bar|Association football}}

[[Category:Liechtenstein national football team| ]]
[[Category:Liechtenstein men's national football teams| ]]
[[Category:European national association football teams]]
